payload.coach_noticed: Your easy pace slows measurably with elevation gain — this has held across 41 of your runs. Yesterday's 10-miler at 8:26/mi with 230ft gain fits the pattern: flatter routes at similar effort would land closer to 8:15/mi.
payload.today_context: You have 4x10min threshold scheduled — 40 minutes at 6:31/mi pace within 10.8 miles total. Given your reported high readiness and no soreness, this is a go. Priority after: fuel within 30 minutes and aim for 7+ hours sleep tonight, as Saturday brings 5x1mi intervals.
payload.week_assessment: You're at 25 miles through Thursday with two quality sessions remaining — this week's trajectory points to a 55-58 mile week, right at your recent peak range, which sets you up to absorb the final 10K sharpening block before taper.
payload.checkin_reaction: Glad you're feeling strong with high readiness and clean legs — let's preserve that by keeping today's threshold controlled and prioritizing recovery fuel and sleep for Saturday's mile repeats.
payload.race_assessment: With 16 days to the Coke 10K, your current fitness supports a sub-40 attempt based on your threshold pace progression and recent 15-miler at 8:31/mi with controlled heart rate. The next 10 days of quality work will determine if you can dip under 39:30.
payload.morning_voice: Your overnight HRV has tracked against your efficiency trend three times in the past week — the effect appears two days later. What do you think is driving this? Meanwhile, you have 4x10min at threshold on deck today, and Saturday brings 5x1mi repeats, so how you recover tonight matters more than the workout itself.
payload.workout_why: This threshold session builds the sustained aerobic power that carries you through the 10K's middle miles when lactate begins to accumulate.
